scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced only evidence-supported hotel operations work to completion and kept unresolved work in active or blocked state with occupancy, room-status, staffing, and ticket evidence.
Missing PMS, housekeeping, work-order, and staffing prerequisites correctly produced a hold and missing-evidence request instead of an unsafe room-release decision.
Pricing, refund, resort-fee, and walk-strategy authority were rejected cleanly and routed to revenue, guest services, or property leadership.
The PMS, CRS, housekeeping, and payment mismatch was treated as a cross-record blocker with front-office, revenue, and issuer-boundary routing rather than forced correction.
A failed room inspection with open maintenance and accessible-room impact moved into rework or hold with the correct housekeeping, maintenance, and front-office escalation.
Completion was refused until inspection, work-order closure, guest-impact, and PMS room-status evidence existed, preserving reversible lifecycle state.
The high-volume group-arrival queue was segmented by readiness, guest impact, authority boundary, and owner without skipping occupancy or service-level evidence.
A water-system and public-health concern triggered safety escalation and guest-list evidence handling while refusing unsupported safety closure.
Identity mismatch, room access, phone-change, and refund-reroute signals triggered front-office and fraud escalation without operational workaround or payment-data exposure.
The lane preserved hotel operations terminology and tied each artifact to the correct system, lifecycle state, and next action.
Conflicting public, brand, property, and safety policies were recorded as a precedence conflict requiring human resolution.
Tenant-specific room-readiness, staffing, labor, PMS, and safety terms were adapted without losing public-baseline constraints or evidence discipline.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.

The package stays inside hotel operations execution and does not claim pricing, refund, compensation, front-office room control, fraud adjudication, safety signoff, engineering clearance, or executive approval authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
